rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical [3+2]/[4+2] radical cascade cyclization between N-methacryloylindoles and α-allyl-substituted methylenes to form indole-fused polycyclics using a Cp2Fe/DMAP mediator system.
electrochemical_role: Anode oxidizes a coordinated Cp2Fe(II)-DMAP complex to Fe(III), which then performs single-electron oxidation of the α-allyl methylene carbanion (generated by HFIP-mediated deprotonation, with H2 evolved at the cathode) to a carbon-centered radical; later anodic/Fe(III) oxidation of a cyclohexadienyl radical to a cation enables rearomatization.

key_intermediates.name: α-allyl methylene carbanion (2-1) from cathodic-driven HFIP deprotonation
Adduct radical 3-1 after first radical addition to N-methacryloylindole alkene
Cyclohexadienyl (indolyl) radical 3-3 after 6-exo-trig cyclization onto indole C2

electron_flow: Cathode reduces HFIP to release H2 and generate an HFIP anion that deprotonates the α-allyl methylene to a carbanion (2-1); concurrently the anode oxidizes the Cp2Fe(II)-DMAP complex to Fe(III), which SET-oxidizes 2-1 to a C-radical (2-2). This radical adds to the methacryloyl alkene, undergoes sequential 5-exo-trig and 6-exo-trig cyclizations onto the indole, and the resulting cyclohexadienyl radical is oxidized (by Fe(III) or anode) to a cation that loses H+ to rearomatize and yield the polycycle.
rate_determining_step: Not the C-H bond cleavage of substrates 1 or 2 (KIE ≈ 0.78 and 1.18 indicate non-RDS C-H steps); RDS not explicitly assigned but consistent with radical addition/cyclization or mediator turnover.

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: No desired product; BHT-radical adduct 31 isolated in 65%, supporting a C-radical intermediate.

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: Product yield dropped to 17%; DPE-trapped adduct 32 isolated in 53%, confirming radical intermediacy.

probe_reagents.name: [D]2-HFIP
probe_reagents.observation: 60% deuterium incorporation at α-position of 2 ([D]-2), indicating rapid reversible C-H cleavage of the α-allyl methylene.

probe_reagents.name: [D/H]-2 (α-deuterated methylene)
probe_reagents.observation: kH/kD ≈ 0.78 (inverse KIE), C-H cleavage of 2 is not rate-determining.

probe_reagents.name: [D/H]-1 (deuterated N-methacryloylindole)
probe_reagents.observation: kH/kD ≈ 1.18, small/negligible KIE — C-H cleavage of 1 not rate-determining.

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of Cp2Fe + DMAP mixture
probe_reagents.observation: New oxidation peak at ~0.7 V (between Cp2Fe and DMAP alone) indicates a coordinated Fe(II)-DMAP species is formed and is the species oxidized at the anode.

notes: Control experiments establish Cp2Fe as the essential mediator (Cu/Mn/Ni salts inactive) and DMAP as critical ligand; replacing DMAP with pyridine or N,N-dimethylaniline severely lowers yield. Reaction tolerant of air and water. Electron-poor indoles (CF3, NO2) unreactive, consistent with the cation/radical addition onto the indole π-system requiring sufficient nucleophilicity. The α-allyl methylene must be disubstituted at the γ-alkene (monosubstituted variants M1-M3 failed), and aryl esters (M4) failed, attributed to instability/steric hindrance of the alkyl radical intermediate.
